questions.statement:
<p><span style="font-weight: bolder;">Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error.</span></p>

<div>The company offered discounts&nbsp;low as / twenty-five percent on selected products /, aiming to attract a broader customer base / and encourage greater sales during the festive season.</div>
options: aiming to attract a broader customer base /
The company offered discounts low as /
and encourage greater sales during the festive season /

questions.statement:
<p><strong>DIRECTIONS :</strong> Read the sentence to find out whether there is any grammatical mistake/error in it. The error, if any, will be in one part of the sentence. Mark that part as your answer. If the sentence is correct, mark (4) (no error) as your answer. (Ignore the errors of punctuation, if any).</p>

<p>I found this toy (1)/while digging in the garden, but (2)/I don&rsquo;t know to whom it belonged to (3)/No error (4)</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">Find the part of the sentence that contains an error:</span></p>

<p>While the committee&rsquo;s proposal to overhaul the tax code was met with significant skepticism, (1) / their primary objectives remain to simplify the filing (2) /&nbsp;process, increasing transparency for middle-income earners, (3) / and to eliminate&nbsp;loopholes that benefit multinational corporations. (4)</p>

<p><strong>Directions: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.</strong></p>

<p>Your name / precedes before / mine / in the list.</p>
Spot the error in following sentence: None of them come to the inauguration of our new factory&nbsp;

<p><strong>Choose the part of the following sentences that is grammatically incorrect :</strong></p>

<p>Charles Dickens is famous as a novelist and a essayist.</p>

<p><strong>Direction: Choose the part of the sentence which has an error.</strong></p>

<p>That Brutus, who was his trusted friend (A)/had attacked (B)/ on him caused (C) / heartbreak to Julius Caesar (D).</p>

<p>Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose &#39;No error&#39;.</p>

<p>The smallest dog barks the loud.&nbsp;</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700; ">Directions: In the following question, some parts of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select &#39;No Error&#39;.</span></p>

<p>The leader of the football players make all decisions for the entire group.</p>
options: <span style="">The leader of the football players</span> /

questions.statement:
<strong>Select the option that contains a grammatical error in the underlined portion.</strong><br />
There are three cupholders placed on the cupboards of the secretariat table.
options: <u>There are three cupholders</u> placed on the cupboards of the secretariat table. /
There are three cupholders placed <u>on the cupboards</u> of the secretariat table. /
There are three cupholders placed on the cupboards of <u>the secretariat table</u> /
There are three <u>cupholders placed </u>on the cupboards of the secretariat table. /
